items.title: Learner driver charged over Supanova expo death at Melbourne showgrounds
items.standfirst: Werribee man, 33, was charged with dangerous driving causing death and culpable driving after a man was struck and killed at Comic Con event Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA learner driver has been charged with killing a pedestrian after a car ploughed into a group outside a popular comic convention.A 20-year-old man died and another man the same age was seriously injured when a Toyota sedan mounted the kerb outside Melbourne Showgrounds in Ascot Vale just before 5pm on Saturday. Continue reading...
items.content: Werribee man, 33, was charged with dangerous driving causing death and culpable driving after a man was struck and killed at Comic Con event Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA learner driver has been charged with killing a pedestrian after a car ploughed into a group outside a popular comic convention.A 20-year-old man died and another man the same age was seriously injured when a Toyota sedan mounted the kerb outside Melbourne Showgrounds in Ascot Vale just before 5pm on Saturday. Continue reading...

items.title: Unlicensed driver charged after 'hitting and killing man outside comic con'
items.standfirst: A learner driver without a licence has been charged after a man was killed and another was seriously hurt outside a comic convention in Melbourne.
items.content: A learner driver without a licence has been charged after a man was killed and another was seriously hurt outside a comic convention in Melbourne.The driver of a Toyota Echo allegedly swerved into a group of people outside the Showgrounds just after 5pm last night, hitting two men in their early 20s.One man from Keysborough in the city's south-west died at the scene.LIVE UPDATES: Arch Trump foe claims president was dragged into war The second man was taken to hospital with significant lower limb injuries, and is fighting for life.A third person in the group was not hit, but has been taken to hospital for shock.It is understood the trio had been at the Supernova Comic Con and Gaming event.Police say the Toyota continued along the footpath, before a female off-duty police officer and member of the public intervened and conducted a citizen's arrest.READ MORE: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea The alleged driver, 33-year-old man man from Werribee has been charged with dangerous driving causing death and culpable driving.He was unlicensed and a learner driver.He was due to appear before Melbourne Magistrates' Court this afternoon.READ MORE: Two charged over shootings in south-western Sydney Organisers issued a statement on Saturday night, saying they were "deeply saddened and distressed by the incident" but vowed to continue the weekend-long convention as planned, including a moment to honour the victims."Supanova will continue to support all Supa-Fans that come through our doors tomorrow morning, and we will be holding a minute's silence in sympathy and solidarity of the affected families and friends at 10am before opening our doors to our event," they said in the statement.CRIME: Diners flee as machete attack at restaurant leaves teen in hospital " They are tragic, tragic circumstances," Detective Inspector Craig McEvoy said. "We are still yet to determine if drugs, alcohol, or speed were involved", he added, noting the circumstances were "extremely unusual"."The vehicle has travelled a significant distance along the footpath, we will try to understand the causation of what's caused the driver to travel that distance along the footpath." IN PICTURES: The photos that stopped the nation over the last two centuries NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Man faces Melbourne court after pedestrian death at comic convention
items.standfirst: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.
items.content: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

clusters.title: Moment rescue crew arrives 24 hours after divers disappeared
clusters.neutral_headline: Moment rescue crew arrives 24 hours after divers disappeared

items.title: Moment rescue crew arrives 24 hours after divers disappeared
items.standfirst: Two men have survived 24 hours floating at sea after strong currents pulled them kilometres from their dive boat off Queensland.
items.content: Two men have survived 24 hours floating at sea after strong currents pulled them kilometres from their dive boat off Queensland.Dan Fitzgerald and Stu Fillman's scuba suits are being credited for helping to keep them alive.The rescue they no doubt believed might never come was caught on camera.READ MORE: Unlicenced driver charged after 'hitting and killing man outside comic con'They spent all day and all night in the deep blue four kilometres from land.They didn't have a life raft, only each other, until they were spotted from the sky this morning."They were glad to see me, you could see the weight come off their shoulders and go, 'Thank God you're here,'" rescue crew officer Alex Carroll said.The men, aged 43 and 45, had been diving at Flat Rock, north of Point Lookout on North Stradbroke Island, when strong currents pulled them away from their boat.Those on board raised the alarm yesterday morning when they didn't return, sparking a multi-agency search for the experienced divers.They were winched to safety by the Westpac Surf Live Saving helicopter almost 24 hours later in remarkably good spirits and good condition.READ MORE: Ben Roberts-Smith makes first statement after release from prison on bailREAD MORE: Arch Trump foe claims president was dragged into war Their dry suits are credited with keeping them warm.Fitzgerald said he was spending time with family after giving them a scare while Fillman sent a message to those who saved them."Thank you to our emergency services teams in aircraft, on the water, or on land for turning this into a happy ending," he said.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Divers survive 24 hours at sea

items.content: Two men have survived 24 hours floating at sea after strong currents pulled them kilometres from their dive boat off Queensland’s North Stradbroke Island.

items.title: Boy dies after being hit by car driven by neighbour in Melbourne
items.standfirst: A seven-year-old boy has died after being hit by a car while riding a scooter outside his home near Melbourne.
items.content: A seven-year-old boy has died after being hit by a car while riding a scooter outside his home in Melbourne.The man behind the wheel was a neighbour who stopped at the scene in Melton and was left traumatised by Saad Masud's death.Another neighbour, Mohammed Chowdhury, said he came out and saw the child lying on the street.READ MORE: Unlicenced driver charged after 'hitting and killing man outside comic con'"Another neighbour was helping him to do CPR," he said.The driver stopped and spoke to police.Saad's uncle Kazi Islam said his parents were "heartbroken" by the death of their son, who he said was "always happy"."We just want everyone's prayers. That's the most important thing, I guess," he said."We forgive him, you know. And of course we pray to God that everybody, we should be very careful."READ MORE: Ben Roberts-Smith makes first statement after release from prison on bailThe driver's wife said he was devastated and spent a sleepless night replaying what happened in his mind.Their own children are of a similar age to Saad and would often play with him outside."This is traumatising for everyone. He carry this story for the rest of his life," Islam said.Police have spoken to the driver, but no charges have been laid.READ MORE: Arch Trump foe claims president was dragged into war

clusters.title: The quiet disappearance of Australia's rainfall observation network
clusters.neutral_headline: The quiet disappearance of Australia's rainfall observation network

items.title: The quiet disappearance of Australia's rainfall observation network
items.standfirst: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.
items.content: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.
